commit.message:
chore(agents): unify repo skill configuration (#2436)

## Summary
- Add repo-level Codex config for SessionStart hooks plus pinned
Context7 and Playwright MCP servers.
- Make `.agents/skills` the canonical repo skill source and project
Claude skills as symlinks.
- Keep Sentry enabled as a skill/plugin in both Claude and Codex: Claude
uses `sentry@claude-plugins-official` plus
`sentry-cli@claude-plugins-official`; Codex uses
`sentry@openai-curated`. Live data access can still use MCP/app/CLI
surfaces, while the Sentry skill carries useful SDK/API and triage
workflow knowledge.
- Add `scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh` and external-skill lock metadata to
validate vendored skill hashes, Claude symlinks, stale symlinks, Sentry
plugin availability, and Codex config. First-party repo skills are
tracked by Git instead of hash-locked.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash -n scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh
scripts/sync-agent-skills.test.sh scripts/hooks/session-start-fetch.sh
scripts/hooks/guard-screenshot-path.sh`
- [x] `bash scripts/sync-agent-skills.test.sh`
- [x] `bash scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh --apply`
- [x] `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- [x] Codex repo MCP smoke with temporary project trust: `context7` and
`playwright` listed from `.codex/config.toml`
- [x] Codex real-config prompt-input smoke: `sentry:sentry` appears from
`sentry@openai-curated` in this repo

chore(agents): unify repo skill configuration (#2436)

## Summary
- Add repo-level Codex config for SessionStart hooks plus pinned
Context7 and Playwright MCP servers.
- Make `.agents/skills` the canonical repo skill source and project
Claude skills as symlinks.
- Keep Sentry enabled as a skill/plugin in both Claude and Codex: Claude
uses `sentry@claude-plugins-official` plus
`sentry-cli@claude-plugins-official`; Codex uses
`sentry@openai-curated`. Live data access can still use MCP/app/CLI
surfaces, while the Sentry skill carries useful SDK/API and triage
workflow knowledge.
- Add `scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh` and external-skill lock metadata to
validate vendored skill hashes, Claude symlinks, stale symlinks, Sentry
plugin availability, and Codex config. First-party repo skills are
tracked by Git instead of hash-locked.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash -n scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh
scripts/sync-agent-skills.test.sh scripts/hooks/session-start-fetch.sh
scripts/hooks/guard-screenshot-path.sh`
- [x] `bash scripts/sync-agent-skills.test.sh`
- [x] `bash scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh --apply`
- [x] `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- [x] Codex repo MCP smoke with temporary project trust: `context7` and
`playwright` listed from `.codex/config.toml`
- [x] Codex real-config prompt-input smoke: `sentry:sentry` appears from
`sentry@openai-curated` in this repo

+[features]
+hooks = true
+plugins = true
+
+[[hooks.SessionStart]]
+matcher = "startup|resume"
+
+[[hooks.SessionStart.hooks]]
+type = "command"
+command = 'bash "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/scripts/hooks/session-start-fetch.sh"'
+timeout = 25
+statusMessage = "Checking branch freshness"
+
+[mcp_servers.context7]
+command = "npx"
+args = ["-y", "@upstash/context7-mcp@3.2.0"]
+
+[mcp_servers.playwright]
+command = "npx"
+args = ["-y", "@playwright/mcp@0.0.76"]
+
+[plugins."sentry@openai-curated"]
+enabled = true

@@ -39,6 +39,16 @@ Monorepo: Next.js frontend (`web/`) + Python FastAPI backend (`services/claw-int
 ## Screenshots
 - Write all UI screenshots (Playwright `browser_take_screenshot`, manual grabs, CDP-debug captures) into `.screenshots/` at the repo root — never the repo root itself. The directory is gitignored (only its `README.md` is tracked); images stay local for validation and are never committed. If a capture is worth keeping, move it into the relevant `docs/` location and reference it there.
 
+## Agent skills and tool config
+- **Shared repo skills live in `.agents/skills/`**. This is the canonical source for ECAP workflow skills used by both Codex and Claude Code (`pr`, `release`, `arch-review`, `telep`, `lark-*`, etc.). Codex reads these repo skills directly.
+- **Claude sees repo skills through `.claude/skills/` symlinks**. Do not maintain a second copy of a repo skill under `.claude/skills`; use `scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh --apply` to repair symlinks when needed. A Claude-only skill needs an explicit documented exception before it can live there as a real directory.
+- **`skills-lock.json` tracks only external vendored skill state**. External skills that the repo depends on are checked in as materialized files under `.agents/skills` and recorded in `skills-lock.json` with source/hash metadata. First-party repo skills are tracked by Git and are not hash-locked there. Do not rely on session startup to install skills.
+- **`npx skills` is only for explicit manual refreshes of vendored skills**. It is not used for Context7, Playwright, Codex plugins, Claude plugins, or normal session startup. After refreshing an external skill, check in both the updated `.agents/skills/...` files and `skills-lock.json`.
+- **Codex repo config lives in `.codex/config.toml`**. It declares Codex project hooks plus repo-level Context7/Playwright MCP servers with pinned package versions. This is separate from Claude's `.claude/settings.json`.
+- **Sentry stays enabled as a plugin skill in both tools**. Claude uses `sentry@claude-plugins-official` plus `sentry-cli@claude-plugins-official`; Codex uses `sentry@openai-curated`. Sentry MCP/app/CLI surfaces handle live data access, while the Sentry skill remains useful for SDK/API knowledge and triage workflow guidance.
+- **No repo `.mcp.json` currently**. Add one only if we need a cross-tool project MCP server with stable command, auth, environment, and failure-mode docs. Context7/Playwright are currently configured per tool (`.codex/config.toml` for Codex, `.claude/settings.json` for Claude).
+- Run `bash scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh --check` after changing any `.agents/skills`, `.claude/skills`, `skills-lock.json`, or `.codex/config.toml` file.
+
 ## Local hooks & the push gate
 Two layers of fast local feedback; CI is always the authoritative gate.
 
@@ -52,6 +62,10 @@ Two layers of fast local feedback; CI is always the authoritative gate.
 - **SessionStart → `session-start-fetch.sh`**: best-effort `git fetch origin main` at session start + reports if the branch is behind. Automates the per-session fetch ritual and surfaces stale-main worktrees. Never blocks (offline just prints a note). (No git equivalent — git has no session-start event.)
 - **PreToolUse(screenshot) → `guard-screenshot-path.sh`**: enforces the `.screenshots/` path convention (see Screenshots above).
 
+**Codex hooks (`.codex/config.toml` → `scripts/hooks/`)** — Codex-session equivalents for shared hook behavior:
+- **SessionStart → `session-start-fetch.sh`**: same freshness check as Claude, scoped to Codex startup/resume. Hook changes require Codex project trust review.
+- Do not run network installers or mutate repo-tracked files from agent session hooks; hooks should only add lightweight context or guard unsafe tool usage.
+
 ## Runtime Clusters
 - Do not assume the business layer and bot control plane share one Kubernetes cluster.
 - Business-layer services include `claw-interface`, `billing-gateway`, `ecap-proxy-service`, etc. In both production and staging, these run in the `ecap` namespace on the `srp-cluster` GKE context (`gke_srpproduct-dc37e_us-east1_srp-cluster-production-us-east1` / `gke_srpstaging_us-east1_srp-cluster-staging-us-east1`).

@@ -16,6 +16,7 @@ Utility scripts for the ECAP workspace.
 | `watch-pr-checks.sh` | Poll a PR's checks until they settle, then exit on the real outcome. Normalizes `gh pr checks` pending exit-code 8 / pending `bucket` shape so waiting doesn't misfire. |
 | `open-chrome-debug-profile.sh` | Launch local Chrome with a dedicated debug profile + CDP for Playwright attach. `--env staging` (default) → `ecap.gensmo.nosay.live` on `127.0.0.1:9222`; `--env production` → `zooclaw.ai` on `9223`; `--localhost [PORT]` → a local dev server (`pnpm dev:staging`) on `9422`; `--anon` adds a never-signed-in profile (+100 to the port). Distinct ports/profiles let every combination run side by side. See `cdp/README.md`. |
 | `sentry_memory_rollup.py` | CI monitor: queries Sentry memory-pressure events, creates a synthetic rollup issue when user-count thresholds are exceeded, and emits a ZooClaw dev-group notification message for `.github/workflows/sentry-memory-rollup.yml`. |
+| `sync-agent-skills.sh` | Validate or repair repo agent skill wiring. `--check` verifies `.agents/skills`, `.claude/skills` symlinks, `skills-lock.json`, and `.codex/config.toml` without writes or network. `--apply` repairs Claude symlinks only. |
 | `worktree.sh` | Create, list, and remove git worktrees for parallel development. |
 
 ## Sentry memory rollup

@@ -0,0 +1,268 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+usage() {
+  cat <<'USAGE'
+Usage: scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh --check|--apply
+
+  --check  Validate repo agent skill state without writing files or using network.
+  --apply  Repair Claude skill symlinks for repo-owned shared skills. Does not install external skills.
+USAGE
+}
+
+mode="${1:-}"
+if [ "$mode" != "--check" ] && [ "$mode" != "--apply" ]; then
+  usage >&2
+  exit 2
+fi
+
+repo_root="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+agents_dir="$repo_root/.agents/skills"
+claude_dir="$repo_root/.claude/skills"
+claude_settings="$repo_root/.claude/settings.json"
+lock_file="$repo_root/skills-lock.json"
+codex_config="$repo_root/.codex/config.toml"
+
+failures=0
+
+note() {
+  printf '%s\n' "$*"
+}
+
+fail() {
+  printf 'agent-skills: %s\n' "$*" >&2
+  failures=$((failures + 1))
+}
+
+require_file() {
+  [ -f "$1" ] || fail "missing file: ${1#$repo_root/}"
+}
+
+toml_section_has_line() {
+  local file="$1"
+  local section="$2"
+  local expected="$3"
+
+  awk -v section="$section" -v expected="$expected" '
+    $0 == section {
+      in_section = 1
+      next
+    }
+    /^\[/ {
+      in_section = 0
+    }
+    in_section && $0 == expected {
+      found = 1
+    }
+    END {
+      exit found ? 0 : 1
+    }
+  ' "$file"
+}
+
+sha256_file() {
+  if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    sha256sum "$1" | cut -d ' ' -f 1
+  elif [ -x /sbin/sha256sum ]; then
+    /sbin/sha256sum "$1" | cut -d ' ' -f 1
+  elif command -v openssl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    openssl dgst -sha256 "$1" | awk '{print $NF}'
+  else
+    return 1
+  fi
+}
+
+hash_skill_dir() {
+  local dir="$1"
+  if [ ! -d "$dir" ]; then
+    fail "cannot hash missing skill dir: ${dir#$repo_root/}"
+    return 1
+  fi
+
+  local manifest
+  manifest="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/agent-skill-hash.XXXXXX")" || {
+    fail "cannot create temp file for hashing"
+    return 1
+  }
+
+  while IFS= read -r file; do
+    local rel="${file#$dir/}"
+    local file_hash
+    file_hash="$(sha256_file "$file")" || {
+      fail "failed to hash file: ${file#$repo_root/}"
+      rm -f "$manifest"
+      return 1
+    }
+    printf '%s  %s\n' "$file_hash" "$rel" >>"$manifest"
+  done < <(find "$dir" -type f -print | LC_ALL=C sort)
+
+  local dir_hash
+  dir_hash="$(sha256_file "$manifest")" || {
+    fail "failed to hash skill manifest for: ${dir#$repo_root/}"
+    rm -f "$manifest"
+    return 1
+  }
+  rm -f "$manifest"
+  printf '%s\n' "$dir_hash"
+}
+
+check_codex_config() {
+  require_file "$codex_config"
+  [ -f "$codex_config" ] || return
+
+  grep -q '^\[features\]$' "$codex_config" || fail ".codex/config.toml missing [features]"
+  toml_section_has_line "$codex_config" "[features]" "hooks = true" \
+    || fail ".codex/config.toml missing hooks feature"
+  toml_section_has_line "$codex_config" "[features]" "plugins = true" \
+    || fail ".codex/config.toml missing plugins feature"
+  grep -q '^\[\[hooks.SessionStart\]\]$' "$codex_config" || fail ".codex/config.toml missing SessionStart hook"
+  grep -q 'session-start-fetch.sh' "$codex_config" || fail ".codex/config.toml missing session-start-fetch hook command"
+  grep -q '^\[mcp_servers.context7\]$' "$codex_config" || fail ".codex/config.toml missing context7 MCP"
+  grep -q '@upstash/context7-mcp@' "$codex_config" || fail "context7 MCP package must be version-pinned"
+  grep -q '^\[mcp_servers.playwright\]$' "$codex_config" || fail ".codex/config.toml missing playwright MCP"
+  grep -q '@playwright/mcp@' "$codex_config" || fail "playwright MCP package must be version-pinned"
+  grep -q '^\[plugins\."sentry@openai-curated"\]$' "$codex_config" || fail ".codex/config.toml missing sentry plugin"
+  toml_section_has_line "$codex_config" '[plugins."sentry@openai-curated"]' "enabled = true" \
+    || fail ".codex/config.toml missing enabled sentry plugin"
+}
+
+check_claude_settings() {
+  require_file "$claude_settings"
+  [ -f "$claude_settings" ] || return
+
+  if ! command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    fail "node not found; cannot validate .claude/settings.json"
+    return
+  fi
+
+  node -e '
+    const settings = JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync(process.argv[1], "utf8"));
+    const plugins = settings.enabledPlugins || {};
+    for (const name of ["sentry@claude-plugins-official", "sentry-cli@claude-plugins-official"]) {
+      if (plugins[name] !== true) {
+        console.error(`missing enabled Claude plugin: ${name}`);
+        process.exit(1);
+      }
+    }
+  ' "$claude_settings" || fail ".claude/settings.json missing required Sentry plugins"
+}
+
+check_lock_file() {
+  require_file "$lock_file"
+  [ -f "$lock_file" ] || return
+
+  if ! command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    fail "node not found; cannot validate skills-lock.json"
+    return
+  fi
+
+  node -e 'JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync(process.argv[1], "utf8"))' "$lock_file" \
+    || fail "skills-lock.json is not valid JSON"
+
+  while IFS= read -r entry; do
+    [ -n "$entry" ] || continue
+    local name source_type repo_path expected actual
+    name="${entry%%	*}"
+    entry="${entry#*	}"
+    source_type="${entry%%	*}"
+    entry="${entry#*	}"
+    repo_path="${entry%%	*}"
+    expected="${entry#*	}"
+
+    if [ "$source_type" = "repo" ]; then
+      fail "skills-lock entry '$name' is repo-owned; first-party skills are tracked by git, not skills-lock.json"
+      continue
+    fi
+    [ -n "$repo_path" ] || {
+      fail "skills-lock entry '$name' missing repoPath"
+      continue
+    }
+    [ -n "$expected" ] || {
+      fail "skills-lock entry '$name' missing materializedHash"
+      continue
+    }
+    [ -d "$repo_root/$repo_path" ] || {
+      fail "locked skill '$name' missing repoPath $repo_path"
+      continue
+    }
+    actual="$(hash_skill_dir "$repo_root/$repo_path")"
+    if [ "$actual" != "$expected" ]; then
+      fail "locked skill '$name' hash mismatch: expected $expected got $actual"
+    fi
+  done < <(
+    node -e '
+      const lock = JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync(process.argv[1], "utf8"));
+      for (const [name, item] of Object.entries(lock.skills || {})) {
+        console.log([name, item.sourceType || "", item.repoPath || "", item.materializedHash || ""].join("\t"));
+      }
+    ' "$lock_file"
+  )
+}
+
+repair_or_check_claude_symlink() {
+  local name="$1"
+  local target="../../.agents/skills/$name"
+  local link="$claude_dir/$name"
+
+  if [ -L "$link" ]; then
+    local actual
+    actual="$(readlink "$link")"
+    if [ "$actual" != "$target" ]; then
+      if [ "$mode" = "--apply" ]; then
+        unlink "$link"
+        ln -s "$target" "$link"
+        note "repaired symlink .claude/skills/$name -> $target"
+      else
+        fail ".claude/skills/$name points to $actual, expected $target"
+      fi
+    fi
+    return
+  fi
+
+  if [ -e "$link" ]; then
+    fail ".claude/skills/$name exists but is not a symlink"
+    return
+  fi
+
+  if [ "$mode" = "--apply" ]; then
+    ln -s "$target" "$link"
+    note "created symlink .claude/skills/$name -> $target"
+  else
+    fail "missing Claude symlink for shared skill '$name'"
+  fi
+}
+
+check_skill_projection() {
+  [ -d "$agents_dir" ] || fail "missing .agents/skills"
+  [ -d "$claude_dir" ] || fail "missing .claude/skills"
+  [ -d "$agents_dir" ] && [ -d "$claude_dir" ] || return
+
+  while IFS= read -r skill_dir; do
+    local name
+    name="$(basename "$skill_dir")"
+    require_file "$skill_dir/SKILL.md"
+    repair_or_check_claude_symlink "$name"
+  done < <(find "$agents_dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -print | LC_ALL=C sort)
+
+  while IFS= read -r link; do
+    local name target
+    name="$(basename "$link")"
+    target="$(readlink "$link")"
+    case "$target" in
+      ../../.agents/skills/*)
+        [ -d "$agents_dir/$name" ] || fail ".claude/skills/$name points to a missing .agents skill"
+        ;;
+    esac
+  done < <(find "$claude_dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type l -print | LC_ALL=C sort)
+}
+
+check_codex_config
+check_claude_settings
+check_lock_file
+check_skill_projection
+
+if [ "$failures" -gt 0 ]; then
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+note "agent-skills: ${mode#--} ok"

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+repo_root="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+cd "$repo_root"
+
+bash scripts/sync-agent-skills.sh --check

@@ -5,13 +5,17 @@
       "source": "larksuite/cli",
       "sourceType": "github",
       "skillPath": "skills/lark-im/SKILL.md",
-      "computedHash": "0fc96d248fd0a26f09123422e32bf93fc47b5642ce8d54cfe5ec5085ea3fe4be"
+      "computedHash": "0fc96d248fd0a26f09123422e32bf93fc47b5642ce8d54cfe5ec5085ea3fe4be",
+      "repoPath": ".agents/skills/lark-im",
+      "materializedHash": "ed0f96f159e552f67b568e4e38b20acf4280df24cb56fb78c3666b60157ec7bc"
     },
     "lark-shared": {
       "source": "larksuite/cli",
       "sourceType": "github",
       "skillPath": "skills/lark-shared/SKILL.md",
-      "computedHash": "8adfe7552fc1124358e61a13b56e1c61f63e237dd0bca5de9aea42c8b28597be"
+      "computedHash": "8adfe7552fc1124358e61a13b56e1c61f63e237dd0bca5de9aea42c8b28597be",
+      "repoPath": ".agents/skills/lark-shared",
+      "materializedHash": "e675710429735486bfd4c0373dd21c993fc54800e5bb5ee06434b3cc7e08adb6"
     }
   }
 }
